name: enact-hello-simple
description: A bare-bones example that prints a greeting
capabilities: Prints a greeting message to the console
Shows how to define a simple command-based tool
Serves as a template for creating new Enact tools
author: EnactProtocol
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The .sigstore-bundle.json references are Sigstore code-signing artifacts, not skill code. High entropy strings are expected certificate data. The skill is a simple echo command that outputs a greeting.
valueStatement: This simple Enact tool demonstrates basic tool usage by printing a greeting message. It serves as a starting point for learning the Enact protocol.
limitations: Only prints a static greeting message
Cannot accept user input or arguments

useCases.title: Learn the Enact protocol
useCases.description: New developers can study this skill to understand how Enact tools are structured and defined.

useCases.title: Quick template starter
useCases.description: Use this as a starting template when building new Enact tools with minimal boilerplate.

useCases.title: Test tool integration
useCases.description: Verify that the Enact tool system is working correctly by running a simple command.

promptTemplates.title: Basic usage
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the enact/hello-simple tool to see a greeting message.

promptTemplates.title: Learning inquiry
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how the hello-simple Enact tool works and explain its structure.

promptTemplates.title: Template request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Enact tool similar to hello-simple but with a custom message.

promptTemplates.title: Integration check
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify the Enact tool system is working by running the hello-simple example.

bestPractices: Start with this simple example when learning Enact tool development
Keep tool commands focused on single, clear operations
Use descriptive names and documentation for discoverability
antiPatterns: Avoid modifying this tool to do complex tasks - it is designed as a simple example
Do not assume this tool can accept dynamic input or arguments
Avoid using this as a template for production tools without adding error handling

faq.question: What does this tool do?
faq.answer: It prints the message 'Hello from Enact!' using the echo command.

faq.question: Can I customize the greeting?
faq.answer: This version is fixed. You can fork it and modify the command to print any message you need.

faq.question: Is this tool safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. This tool only runs a simple echo command with a hardcoded message and has no file system or network access.

faq.question: What is the Enact protocol?
faq.answer: Enact is a protocol for defining tools that AI agents can use. This skill is a minimal example of an Enact tool.

faq.question: How do I create my own tool like this?
faq.answer: Study this example's SKILL.md file. Define a name, version, description, and command in the YAML frontmatter.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: This skill supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the Enact protocol integration.
